task_completion.description: Verify that the calendar or schedule view is open for Dr. Sarah Chen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Sarah Chen' selected in a provider dropdown, displayed in the calendar header, or visible in the scheduling interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the calendar is navigated to the target date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the date 'March 20, 2026', '03/20/2026', or '2026-03-20' in the date picker, calendar header, or schedule view.

task_completion.description: Verify that a schedule block is created with the correct start and end times.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a calendar event spanning from 08:00 AM to 12:00 PM on the grid, or an appointment creation form showing 08:00 (or 8:00 AM) as the start time and 12:00 (or 12:00 PM) as the end time.

task_completion.description: Verify that the block is administrative and has no patient assigned.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the event category being set to something like 'In Office', 'Provider', or 'Administrative', and ensure there is no patient name attached to this specific schedule block.

task_completion.description: Verify that the schedule block includes the required reason.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Hospital Board Meeting' (or the keywords 'hospital', 'board', 'meeting') in the event title, description, or notes field of the schedule block.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to create the schedule block.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser or application GUI being interacted with (e.g., clicking dropdowns, filling forms), rather than a terminal window executing scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction is with a genuine scheduling/medical application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface should look like a functional scheduling system or EMR (Electronic Medical Record) software, not a plain text editor or a locally mocked HTML file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI using direct database commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no visible terminal windows running SQL queries (e.g., INSERT INTO schedule...) or shell scripts to artificially inject the event into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Provider Name: Sarah Chen. Target Date: 2026-03-20 (March 20, 2026). Start Time: 08:00. End Time: 12:00. Required Keywords in Note: 'hospital', 'board', 'meeting'. The block must be administrative, meaning no patient is assigned.
